title: Expand to Southeast Asia
question: Should we expand our SaaS product into Southeast Asia in Q1 2025?
context: We are a B2B SaaS company with 200 enterprise clients in India. Revenue is $5M ARR growing at 40% YoY. We have received inbound interest from 12 companies in Singapore and Malaysia. Our product supports English and requires some localization effort.
constraints: Budget limited to $500K for market entry. No local entity established. Engineering team is at full capacity. Timeline: must decide in 30 days.
stakes: High — this decision affects 2026 revenue targets and investor narrative. Wrong move could dilute focus from core India market.

title: Migrate to Microservices
question: Should we migrate our monolithic backend to microservices architecture?
context: Our platform handles 50K daily active users. The monolith has 800K lines of code written over 5 years. We experience 2-3 outages per month. Our engineering team of 15 is struggling with deployment velocity — releases take 2 weeks.
constraints: Cannot afford more than 2 weeks of reduced feature output. Must maintain 99.9% uptime during migration. Team has limited microservices experience.
stakes: High — architecture decision will affect engineering productivity for next 3 years. Wrong choice risks increasing technical debt or causing major outages.

title: Hire VP of Sales
question: Should we hire a VP of Sales externally or promote our top Sales Manager internally?
context: Our Sales Manager has been with us 3 years, knows the product deeply, but has never managed a team larger than 4. The external candidate has 10 years experience scaling SaaS sales from $5M to $50M ARR but wants $280K base + equity.
constraints: Hiring budget max $250K total comp. Decision needed within 3 weeks — Q4 pipeline is at risk. Board is watching closely.
stakes: Very High — this hire will define our go-to-market for the next 3 years. Wrong choice risks missing ARR targets and losing the Sales Manager.

title: Launch Freemium Tier
question: Should we launch a freemium tier for our B2B product?
context: We currently charge $500/month minimum. Churn rate is 8% monthly. Top of funnel is weak — only 200 trials/month converting at 12%. Competitors Notion and Monday.com have free tiers. Our infrastructure cost per user is $3/month.
constraints: Must not cannibalize existing paid customers. Engineering effort capped at 6 weeks. No additional headcount approved.
stakes: Medium — freemium could 10x trial volume or create support burden that slows enterprise focus. Affects 2025 growth strategy.

title: Acquire Competitor
question: Should we acquire SmartAnalytics Inc., a direct competitor with 80 clients?
context: SmartAnalytics has $800K ARR, 80 clients, 6 engineers, and a complementary analytics module we lack. Asking price is $4M (5x ARR). Their client overlap with us is 15%. Their tech stack is different (PHP vs our Python).
constraints: Max acquisition budget $3.5M. Integration must complete within 6 months. Our M&A experience is limited.
stakes: Very High — acquisition of wrong asset at wrong price could consume 18 months of leadership attention and $3.5M capital.
